findings.summary: No direct contradiction found between PLAN.md and PHASE-XX.md.
The goal asks for a session-renewal API, but the only phase covers login remember-me duration.
findings.remediation: Either add a phase that defines and implements the renewal API surface, including route or handler, request and response contract, and tests, or revise the goal so it only asks for remember-me session duration.
findings.why_real_risk: An engineer following PHASE-XX can complete the checkbox-based login change without adding any renewal endpoint or callable API behavior. That leaves the stated deliverable absent while the plan still appears traceable.
findings.repro: Dispatch Wave 1 as written. The engineer edits src/auth/login.ts so remember-me login sessions last longer, then marks PHASE-XX complete. A client then attempts to call the requested renewal API and no route, handler, or contract exists.
tests_to_add: Add a plan-validation fixture where a goal requires an API but phases only alter login flow, and assert validation reports an uncovered API output.
Add acceptance criteria requiring a renewal API contract or an explicit goal rewrite when the work is limited to remember-me login duration.

findings.summary: PHASE-XX says remember-me sessions should last longer, but gives no target duration.
findings.remediation: Replace longer with a concrete duration and unit, such as 30 days, and state whether the value applies to cookie max-age, server-side session expiry, refresh-token expiry, or all relevant session stores.
findings.why_real_risk: Different engineers can implement incompatible behavior while satisfying the wording: 24 hours, 30 days, or one year could all be described as longer than the current default.
findings.repro: Engineer A sets remember-me sessions to 24 hours because that is longer than the current default. Engineer B later expects 30 days for the same checkbox. Both implementations match the phase text, so tests and product behavior diverge.
tests_to_add: Add an auth test asserting the exact remember-me session duration after login.
Add a plan-validation fixture that rejects relative duration wording without a concrete numeric target.

findings.summary: Wave 1 write-scope disjointness was checked; only PHASE-XX writes files, so no intra-wave write intersection exists.
findings.repro: Wave 1 contains a single phase, PHASE-XX, with writes limited to src/auth/login.ts. There is no second phase in the same wave that can concurrently write src/auth/login.ts or any other declared write path.

findings.summary: FIX-AMBIG is mapped to one phase, but that phase does not fully satisfy the stated session-renewal API goal.
findings.remediation: Update requirements traceability so FIX-AMBIG maps to explicit deliverables for the renewal API and remember-me duration, or narrow FIX-AMBIG to the remember-me duration decision only.
findings.why_real_risk: The traceability table can give a false completion signal even though the API portion of the goal has no implementation task.
findings.repro: A reviewer checks the Requirements traceability table, sees FIX-AMBIG mapped to PHASE-XX, and accepts the plan. PHASE-XX then changes only src/auth/login.ts, leaving no renewal API behavior to verify.
tests_to_add: Add a traceability validation case requiring every stated goal noun, including API deliverables, to appear in at least one phase task or acceptance criterion.

findings.summary: No architectural-fit issue found in the reviewed plan and phase.
No duplication or needless abstraction issue found in the reviewed plan and phase.
No professionalism issue found in the reviewed plan and phase.
